evals.name: greenfield-no-issue
evals.prompt: I need to build a notifications system that sends email digests to users when they have unread messages. Can you help me get this started?
evals.expected_output: Orchestrator identifies no existing issue (step 1a), offers to create one (1b/1c), then proceeds to spec writing (2b). Should NOT jump straight to coding.

assertions.text: identifies_no_issue: Response recognizes no existing JIRA issue and offers to create one
starts_at_initialization: Response begins at phase 1 (issue tracking), not jumping to implementation
asks_before_creating: Response confirms with user before creating the issue (human-oriented, not auto-creating)

evals.name: existing-issue-no-spec
evals.prompt: Pick up ENG-234 — it's got a title but not much else in the description. Let's flesh it out.
evals.expected_output: Orchestrator loads the issue (step 1a → found), detects the description lacks a structured spec (step 2a → no spec), and proceeds to write a spec (2b). Should ask clarifying questions about the work rather than jumping to implementation.

assertions.text: loads_issue_first: Response indicates fetching/loading the JIRA issue before taking action
identifies_missing_spec: Response recognizes the issue description is bare and needs a spec
proceeds_to_spec_writing: Response moves toward writing a spec, not jumping to planning or coding

evals.name: spec-exists-needs-plan
evals.prompt: PROJ-789 has a solid spec already. I want to figure out how we're going to build it.
evals.expected_output: Orchestrator loads the issue (1a → found), sees the spec exists (2a → yes), checks for a plan (3a → no plan), and proceeds to create an implementation plan (3b). Should NOT rewrite the spec.

assertions.text: skips_spec_writing: Response does NOT attempt to rewrite or revalidate the existing spec
identifies_missing_plan: Response recognizes that an implementation plan is needed
proceeds_to_planning: Response moves to create an implementation plan (step 3b)

evals.name: ready-to-implement
evals.prompt: OK the plan for PROJ-789 looks great, let's start coding.
evals.expected_output: Orchestrator recognises this as plan approval (step 3d → approved), updates the issue status (3e), and begins implementation (4a). Should NOT ask to rewrite the plan or spec.

assertions.text: recognizes_approval: Response treats the user message as plan approval and moves forward
updates_status: Response mentions updating the JIRA issue status to In Progress
begins_implementation: Response starts coding/implementation, does not re-plan or re-spec

evals.name: implementation-done-needs-pr
evals.prompt: I've finished implementing the changes for PROJ-789, can you open a PR?
evals.expected_output: Orchestrator identifies work is at the end of implementation phase, proceeds to draft a PR (4b), then pauses for user review (4c). Should link the PR to the JIRA issue.

assertions.text: proceeds_to_pr: Response moves to draft a PR (step 4b)
links_to_issue: Response mentions linking the PR back to the JIRA issue
pauses_for_review: Response indicates the user should review the PR before proceeding (step 4c)

evals.name: pr-feedback-loop
evals.prompt: The PR looks mostly good but the error handling in the webhook controller is too aggressive — it swallows errors silently. Can you fix that and also add a test for the retry logic?
evals.expected_output: Orchestrator identifies this as PR feedback (step 4c → not approved, go to 4d), addresses the specific feedback, then returns to 4c for re-review. Should NOT create a new PR or update the JIRA status yet.

assertions.text: addresses_feedback: Response focuses on the specific error handling and test feedback
does_not_restart: Response does NOT create a new PR, update JIRA status to Done, or go back to planning
returns_to_review: After addressing feedback, response indicates returning to PR review

evals.name: ready-for-review
evals.prompt: The PR is approved and merged for PROJ-789. Let's wrap this up.
evals.expected_output: Orchestrator moves to review phase: updates JIRA status (5a) and notifies the team on Slack (5b). Should ask which Slack channel to notify if not obvious.

assertions.text: updates_status_to_done: Response mentions transitioning the JIRA issue to Done or In Review
notifies_on_slack: Response mentions sending a Slack notification to the team
links_both_artifacts: Response references both the PR and the JIRA issue in the wrap-up
